channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: SpaceX Finalizes IPO Price at $135 a Share in World’s Largest Public Offering
item.description: Elon Musk’s rocket company said it would sell more than 555 million shares at $135 each in its blockbuster initial public offering, which is set to begin trading on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac
item.media:description: SpaceX will begin trading publicly on Friday under the ticker symbol SPCX.

item.title: Skeptics Question Whether SpaceX Is Worth $1.77 Trillion
item.description: Elon Musk’s rocket company is spending big and losing money. That has raised questions about whether it can justify its valuation for its blockbuster initial public offering.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac and Mike Isaac
item.media:description: SpaceX has set an initial public offering price of $135 a share, which would value it at $1.77 trillion. Some investors are skeptical that the valuation is justified.

item.title: Absent From the SpaceX and OpenAI I.P.O.s? Chinese Investors.
item.description: SpaceX will not raise money from investors in China and Hong Kong. Others firms, like OpenAI, may follow suit.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson, Maureen Farrell and Lauren Hirsch
item.media:description: A Starship test flight at the SpaceX’s Starbase in Texas last month. Investors in China are expected to be excluded from the SpaceX initial public offering.

item.title: The SpaceX Shareholder at the Helm of a Multibillion-Dollar Payout
item.description: Justin Fishner-Wolfson, long a minor figure in Elon Musk’s orbit, is at the helm of a multibillion-dollar payout
item.dc:creator: Rob Copeland
item.media:credit: Aaron Wojack for The New York Times
item.media:description: Justin Fishner-Wolfson’s firm now owns more than 1 percent of SpaceX, worth roughly $20 billion at the company’s expected $1.77 trillion valuation at its listing price.

item.title: Trump Picks Jay Clayton for Director of National Intelligence After Backlash Over Bill Pulte
item.description: The president said he would nominate Jay Clayton, the U.S. attorney in Manhattan and the former chairman of the Securities and Exchange Commission, for the permanent role.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Jonah E. Bromwich, Devlin Barrett and Benjamin Weiser
item.category: Office of the Director of National Intelligence
item.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump’s pick to be the director of national intelligence, Jay Clayton, took over as the U.S. attorney for Manhattan in April 2025.

item.title: House Rejects Bill to Extend Surveillance Power With FISA Section 702 Set to Expire
item.description: A measure to temporarily continue a key surveillance law failed in the House, and members left for a weeklong recess, making an expiration all but inevitable.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison and Dustin Volz
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: “We cannot allow that to go dark,” Speaker Mike Johnson told reporters on Wednesday as prospects for the law being renewed appeared increasingly grim.

item.title: Trump and Hegseth Broadcast U.S. Military Strikes Before They Happen
item.description: Commanders do not usually speak publicly about future operations to avoid jeopardizing the mission’s success.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump said he was telegraphing the American strikes to pressure Iran to reach a deal to open the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Oil Prices Drop as Trump Reverses Threat to Strike Iran
item.description: Oil prices jumped after the United States and Iran exchanged fire for a second day, heightening fears that the two countries could slide back into open conflict.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Iran’s Attacks on Gulf States Underscore Targeted Countries’ Dependence on U.S.
item.description: The targeted countries host sprawling American military bases and thousands of personnel at a time when the United States is waging war against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A mural in Tehran, seen on Monday.

item.title: Justice Dept. Pushes Limits of Its Power Over State Elections
item.description: The department once tried to stay out of state elections, urging caution. It is now pressing forward with claims of fraud as President Trump revives his unfounded assertions that elections cannot be trusted.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett
item.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
item.media:description: Voters casting their ballots in California’s primary races in Los Angeles last week.

item.title: Postal Service Issues Proposal to Block Mail Ballots in States That Don’t Turn Over Data
item.description: Democrats and voting-rights groups have challenged the proposed rule as a harmful federal intrusion that could affect millions of voters who cast their ballots by mail.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella and Nick Corasaniti
item.media:description: Department of Elections workers sort mail-in ballots for the California primary election at City Hall in San Francisco, Calif., earlier this month.

item.title: 30 Shows to Watch This Summer
item.description: “The Bear” serves its last course, “Ted Lasso” gets back on the pitch, and Larry David makes a TV show with the Obamas.
item.dc:creator: Mike Hale
item.media:description: “Ted Lasso” returns in August with, center, from left, Brendan Hunt, Jason Sudeikis and Tanya Reynolds.

item.title: After Senate Loss, Cornyn Predicts ‘Miserable’ Final Two Years for Trump
item.description: In his first extensive interview since his defeat by a Trump-backed challenger, the Texas Republican said the Senate was in for a “bumpy ride” as he and others flex new political freedom.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse
item.media:credit: Pete Marovich for The New York Times
item.media:description: “There’s never going to be good enough for him, other than 100 percent, you know, slavish adherence to whatever he wants,” Senator John Cornyn said of President Trump. “But obviously that’s not what the senator’s role is supposed to be.”

item.title: Conor McGregor’s Comeback: A Tale of Banned Drugs and a Famous Doctor
item.description: McGregor, the Ultimate Fighting Championship’s main attraction, had the support of the prominent sports physician Neal ElAttrache when he decided to use performance-enhancing drugs.
item.dc:creator: Michael S. Schmidt
item.media:description: Conor McGregor preparing to fight Dustin Poirier in Las Vegas in 2021. McGregor suffered a badly broken leg during the bout.

item.title: Trump Unveils UFC ‘Claw’ Ahead of Cage Match at the White House
item.description: The towering claw will be the site of an Ultimate Fighting Championship cage match on Sunday, which is President Trump’s 80th birthday.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh

item.title: Can’t Pay Medical Bills? Trump Administration Suggests Getting a Loan
item.description: One-third of Americans shoulder health care debt. Insurers are being asked to consider lending money to Obamacare consumers who can’t afford higher deductibles.
item.dc:creator: Reed Abelson
item.category: Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (2010)
item.media:credit: Cig Harvey for The New York Times
item.media:description: Kathleen Capetta’s family of five is paying an additional $750 a month this year for its Obamacare plan premium. She was already paying off debt from cancer treatment.

item.title: In the U.K., a Violent Cycle: Hateful Attacks, Right-Wing Agitation and Riots
item.description: Two stabbings have fueled violent protests in England and Northern Ireland in the last 10 days, fanned online by right-wing voices. In Britain and across Europe, it’s a grim pattern.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Protests north of Belfast, Northern Ireland, on Wednesday in response to a stabbing attack two days earlier.

item.title: The Researcher Who Didn’t Want to Know
item.description: Her decades of work on Huntington’s disease helped lead to the creation of a genetic test for the devastating condition. Why didn’t she take it herself?
item.dc:creator: Gina Kolata
item.media:credit: Jackie Molloy for The New York Times
item.media:description: Nancy Wexler a lifelong researcher of Huntington’s disease, who has the condition herself, in her Manhattan apartment.

item.title: Mamdani Finds a Foil Familiar to New Yorkers: James Dolan
item.description: Mr. Dolan, the owner of the New York Knicks, began a public feud with Mayor Zohran Mamdani with his team on the verge of an N.B.A. title.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rubinstein
item.media:description: James Dolan suggested that the incompetence of Mayor Zohran Mamdani and the police commissioner, Jessica Tisch, left them incapable of handling “anything like this.”

item.title: This Is What Joy Looks Like for Knicks Fans
item.description: A thrilling, historic comeback victory in Game 4 of the N.BA. finals sent Madison Square Garden, and the rest of New York City, into a lasting frenzy.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar
item.media:credit: Shuran Huang for The New York Times
item.media:description: A celebration at Wayne and Sons in Brooklyn as the Knicks roared back from a 29-point deficit to win Game 4 of the N.B.A. finals at Madison Square Garden.

item.title: NOAA Issues El Nino Advisory
item.description: The global weather pattern threatens to worsen floods and heat waves already intensifying due to climate change. But it may also mean fewer hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Chico Harlan
item.media:description: Drought-stressed wheat in a field in Kansas last month.

item.title: Millions Across the Midwest and Northeast Face Severe Storms on Thursday
item.description: For the second day in a row, Chicago is in the bull’s-eye of the areas facing severe weather on Thursday, but the storm forecast is widespread.
item.dc:creator: Judson Jones

item.title: Heat in the Northeast Thursday and Friday Brings the Threat of Dangerous Storms
item.description: Soupy, hot weather will settle into the Northeast on Thursday and Friday, bringing with it the threat of dangerous storms.
item.dc:creator: Judson Jones
item.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
item.media:description: A security guard in New York cooling down during a hot day last summer. This week will bring the year’s first widespread dose of summerlike heat and humidity to the city.

item.title: Inside Trump’s White House, the Epstein Files Caused a Freakout
item.description: The president’s top advisers gathered in a series of Situation Room meetings as they struggled to contain a scandal engulfing Donald Trump himself.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan
item.category: Regime Change: Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump (Book)

item.title: The Big Questions About Jeffrey Epstein: What The Times Has Learned
item.description: Here is what we know about the sexual predator whose secrets spurred an international reckoning over money, power and complicity. Check back for updates.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Scientists Measure Earth’s Vast Underground Fungal Webs
item.description: With machine learning and a high-resolution imaging robot, scientists measured and mapped the extent of Earth’s carbon circulatory system.
item.dc:creator: Emily Anthes

item.title: Tom Steyer Spent $558 Million. What a Waste.
item.description: Imagine what Tom Steyer could have done with all the money he wasted on another campaign.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Cottle
item.media:credit: Michael Schmelling for The New York Times

item.title: ‘All Men Are Created Equal’? Not Everyone Agrees.
item.description: Our founding creed has always had its critics.
item.dc:creator: Kim Phillips-Fein

item.title: U.S. Blocks Deal by Florida-based Vanguard Energy to Supply Fuel to Cuba
item.description: The deal to ship 250,000 barrels of fuel to Cuba could have eased an energy crisis. But the Trump administration says Vanguard Energy lacks the authorization to proceed.
item.dc:creator: Simon Romero
item.media:description: A blackout in Havana on Wednesday.
